scope_note: QA Harmonic Cycle Platonic Inscription cert [327]. Primary source: Iverson, B. (1991) Quantum Arithmetic Volume II -- Books 3 & 4: New Wave Theory / Synchronous Harmonics. Delta Spectrum Research, La Junta CO. pp.10-12 'MULTIPLE WAVES', 'THE HARMONIC CYCLE', 'OTHER CYCLES', 'MOST BASIC MULTIPLE CYCLE'. Five structural results certified: (C1) 60-unit harmonic cycle = lcm(3,4,5) = 3x4x5; all three waves (3,4,5) pairwise coprime -- gcd(3,4)=gcd(4,5)=gcd(3,5)=1. (C2) Pair (3,4): lcm=12; synchronous points in 60-unit cycle = {12,24,36,48,60}; 5 equally spaced points inscribe a regular pentagon -- the face of icosahedron and dodecahedron. (C3) Pair (3,5): lcm=15; synchronous points = {15,30,45,60}; 4 equally spaced points inscribe a square -- the face of the cube. (C4) Pair (4,5): lcm=20; synchronous points = {20,40,60}; 3 equally spaced points inscribe an equilateral triangle -- the face of tetrahedron, octahedron, icosahedron. (C5) Four fundamental harmonic cycles: 30=lcm(2,3,5), 42=lcm(2,3,7), 60=lcm(3,4,5), 105=lcm(3,5,7); each is the LCM of three pairwise-coprime prime-wave periods. Iverson: 'Connecting these in-phase (Synchronous) points for each pair will inscribe a pentagon, a square, and an equilateral triangle respectively. These happen to be the three plane shapes which compose the five different Platonic solids.' Builds on cert [326] (Euclid Four Par Types / synchronous period). DOI: primary text, no external DOI.
